| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | First Invеstmеnt Updаtеs brоught tо уоu bу Іnception Media, LLC. We would like to emphasize that we do not offer tailored financial advice. This email does not serve as financial guidance, and you bear full responsibility for your investment decisions. name: Chelsey Wininger
subject: Reaching out to you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Democratic Association of Secretaries of State, Treasurer Denise Merrill
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130156790/616811002/-664395954?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYTc1N2JkOS1iZjQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3KBvHhlHOz-QFWaXzXD0F6RvDQ3UyyH3seoh9cZYXZQ=)
Hi there, Chelsey Wininger here, DASS Executive Director. I want to be direct about why Secretary of State races matter so much right now. These are the officials responsible for overseeing elections in their states – from maintaining voter rolls to ensuring ballots are counted and results are certified. They’re also the ones who help make sure elections are run fairly, that voters can access the ballot, and that results are upheld. At DASS, we support Democratic Secretaries of State because they’re committed to doing this work the right way – protecting voters, supporting election workers, and making sure every eligible vote is counted. And if we lose these races, those roles could be filled by election deniers. **That means the people overseeing elections in key states – including the 2028 presidential election – could be the same ones who have questioned or refused to accept election results.** That’s what’s at stake this year. We’re working to elect and re-elect leaders who will keep our elections fair and secure – not what Trump and his MAGA allies want. [**But these are competitive races, and building the resources to win them takes grassroots support. If you can, chip in $20 today to help us elect Democratic Secretaries of State nationwide.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130156791/616811003/-209663061?refcode=em-4.25.26-all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYTc1N2JkOS1iZjQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3KBvHhlHOz-QFWaXzXD0F6RvDQ3UyyH3seoh9cZYXZQ=) __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: __ | | | **[Chip in $5 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130156792/616811004/-209663061?refcode=em-4.25.26-all&express_lane=true&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYTc1N2JkOS1iZjQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3KBvHhlHOz-QFWaXzXD0F6RvDQ3UyyH3seoh9cZYXZQ=)**

clean_body: | = Hi there, Chelsey Wininger here, DASS Executive Director. I want to be direct about why Secretary of State races matter so much right now. These are the officials responsible for overseeing elections in their states – from maintaining voter rolls to ensuring ballots are counted and results are certified. They’re also the ones who help make sure elections are run fairly, that voters can access the ballot, and that results are upheld. At DASS, we support Democratic Secretaries of State because they’re committed to doing this work the right way – protecting voters, supporting election workers, and making sure every eligible vote is counted. And if we lose these races, those roles could be filled by election deniers. That means the people overseeing elections in key states – including the 2028 presidential election – could be the same ones who have questioned or refused to accept election results. That’s what’s at stake this year. We’re working to elect and re-elect leaders who will keep our elections fair and secure – not what Trump and his MAGA allies want. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = ** | = | | = | = | | = | = ** Thank you, Chelsey Wininger Executive Director | Thank you for your dedicated support of The Democratic Association of Secretaries of State -- the only organization dedicated to electing and protecting Democratic Secretaries of States. Peter, anti-democracy forces and pundits in the media think Democrats and the fair maps movement should give up on the South. The more that these states trend Republican, the more some people want us to retreat. We refuse to do that. **The NDRC exists exactly for this reason: To level the playing field** , especially for voters of color in these states who should have the right to elect the representatives of their choice. There’s reason for optimism, especially at this moment: In recent elections, Democrats have pulled off stunning upsets in deep red areas. Even Donald Trump's home district at Mar-a-Lago in Florida is now represented by a Democrat in the state legislature. Every voter in the South deserves a voice — and we won’t stop our efforts to ensure that those voices are heard. [**But we can’t do it alone. Your support is needed to strengthen our fight for fair representation in the South and across the country.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130156632/616810700/359562654?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_TheSouth_email_20260425-NDRC-Tier1-TheSouth_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260425-NDRC-Tier1-TheSouth_&utm_campaign=TheSouth&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjA5NzU3YmQ5LWJmNDAtZjExMS04ZWYyLTAwMGQzYTE0YjY0MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=LGWDeflM90_4qBGJJ9r7fmVs94jvVk-aKQewal6vOF8=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

The civil rights movement took root in states like Alabama, Mississippi, Louisiana, and Florida, where tireless advocates like Medgar Evers and Fannie Lou Hamer gave everything they had to ensure that Black people were able to vote. Thanks to their sacrifices, the Voting Rights Act was established to protect voters of color in America.
**Here’s the urgent truth: The Supreme Court will soon decide on whether or not key parts of the Voting Rights Act will remain in effect.** This decision could affect the voting rights of millions of Americans, particularly in the South.
The fair maps movement has fought for years — on the ground and in court — to preserve this critical piece of legislation. We’re prepared to continue the fight regardless of the outcome.
**And, no matter what, we will not give up on the South.[With your gift now, the NDRC can fight even harder to defend the rights of voters in America and respond to gerrymanders that deny fair representation.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130156661/616810732/359562654?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_TheSouth_email_20260425-NDRC-Tier1-TheSouth_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260425-NDRC-Tier1-TheSouth_&utm_campaign=TheSouth&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjA5NzU3YmQ5LWJmNDAtZjExMS04ZWYyLTAwMGQzYTE0YjY0MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=LGWDeflM90_4qBGJJ9r7fmVs94jvVk-aKQewal6vOF8=)**
Thank you for protecting every voter,
Claire Low
Director of Political and Campaigns
National Democratic Redistricting Committee

clean_body: Together, we will defend the voting rights of Americans across the country. | Peter, anti-democracy forces and pundits in the media think Democrats and the fair maps movement should give up on the South. The more that these states trend Republican, the more some people want us to retreat. We refuse to do that. The NDRC exists exactly for this reason: To level the playing field , especially for voters of color in these states who should have the right to elect the representatives of their choice. There’s reason for optimism, especially at this moment: In recent elections, Democrats have pulled off stunning upsets in deep red areas. Even Donald Trump's home district at Mar-a-Lago in Florida is now represented by a Democrat in the state legislature. Every voter in the South deserves a voice — and we won’t stop our efforts to ensure that those voices are heard. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = The civil rights movement took root in states like Alabama, Mississippi, Louisiana, and Florida, where tireless advocates like Medgar Evers and Fannie Lou Hamer gave everything they had to ensure that Black people were able to vote. Thanks to their sacrifices, the Voting Rights Act was established to protect voters of color in America. Here’s the urgent truth: The Supreme Court will soon decide on whether or not key parts of the Voting Rights Act will remain in effect. This decision could affect the voting rights of millions of Americans, particularly in the South. The fair maps movement has fought for years — on the ground and in court — to preserve this critical piece of legislation. We’re prepared to continue the fight regardless of the outcome. And, no matter what, we will not give up on the South.= Thank you for protecting every voter, Claire Low Director of Political and Campaigns National Democratic Redistricting Committee = = | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC. Text MAPS to 36787, STOP to end. Msg and Data rates may apply

| | Friend, **Florida Governor Ron DeSantis has called a special session of the Florida legislature to redraw the state's congressional maps — which will meet this week.** His new voting maps would hand Trump up to five additional Republican seats in the U.S. House by weakening the voting power of Black and Latino communities. This is illegal under Florida's own constitution, which bans redrawing maps for partisan gain. But in true fashion of a Trump lackey, DeSantis is doing it anyway. Here's the good news: 56% of Floridians oppose redrawing the maps, members of the state’s congressional caucus are [opposed](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130160610/616818989/1235173450?refcode=emm_ffa_20260425_T1_FLmobz_v1s3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMmI1ZjljYS1jMjQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gJu_XjFqv6Nb6qWWd7ED1upnXbxLO6iKkyTjMMCi-To=&emci=835985c4-c240-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=22b5f9ca-c240-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=1037277), and state lawmakers are already showing hesitation. The maps still have to pass both chambers of Florida’s legislature before DeSantis can sign them. **That means we can stop this.** **Here’s what YOU can do to help fight back against the partisan power grab happening in Florida:** 📱**Sign up to volunteer from anywhere in the country!** We need volunteers to phone bank and encourage Florida voters to push calls into the state legislature. [You can sign up for a shift here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130160612/616818991/1484435608?refcode=emm_ffa_20260425_T1_FLmobz_v1s3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMmI1ZjljYS1jMjQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gJu_XjFqv6Nb6qWWd7ED1upnXbxLO6iKkyTjMMCi-To=&emci=835985c4-c240-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=22b5f9ca-c240-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=1037277) Fair maps mean fair representation for every Floridian. Let's make sure lawmakers hear that loud and clear. In solidarity, The Fair Fight Action Team

body:
Communists, Marxists, Islamists, and Socialists want to make us more like Europe. |
Why do we continue to import people who hate us, Fellow Patriot?

Our immigration system has been cynically used to disadvantage American workers' competitiveness in favor of mass importing from the third world.

It's led to higher crime and lower wages. But that's not the only problem.

We're importing people who are hostile and downright fundamentally opposed to the American values that make our country great.

Just look at what Communists, Marxists, Islamists, and Socialists have done to Europe. We cannot let that happen here at home.

**[Fellow Patriot, I need you in this fight.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=4d8a2eef0c7f4317b962b0bfc6db19c1&_e=OD33qSfwFOHqGpBKksieX5h8DmTduBDwkGLGc_ENCAqUPNTTlytSbvjt7yAuQ3ZFh95N-nwCO8SZEnVsCYdGA_TuwyEHbxnI65gOwL1EfZdqqUARW8XVb4vh6SqavMJwsG24I6AbQyMeu9GoqTyQo6TS2ovBOf8NCVfZgz5cqYsIJv-75REXGUigACKAu90Dl7do2a25WBgp3jaa2K0DsLkqiblbSiQ3K9mgt7l86YFN-n3pmwCOfgnOLyScRlkAFudE5iz7kwEBtbNORlpJgdEKWyMJBbAdKWJ3RscWPNO-L4uLcUUP65KgggtESSpDvNm3E32zcNk1wXOunmiblaHQ93DSTfK9ZomUx_uskNKWjRM9OuJoH5ZHS3JqQ--864f_DXMbrsCCCenaxwm7dYokkndh3egXbpIx39ZfNMkmhZFPJSIV05y4J9mK9g8Y6iRrAmtrcUo4HhyEX--w6XBMX6aVlyXZ7y2_6_0uaG09gwz8UXaqsZhhcyDV3CTNlpMJ5z_U6RSLwa66z77tzhuYJSs-6Fgil1xrclxdWu1IjvJ01iR9R1h9jj-rsTUWJAaLsSj7iU57ur4RjeYTWv54SKfkE2kmmsvf21CVuXzQp-zzJ5lFso5KOja3MVJIG2rctmr258I8OAKlSNd4CQ6ouip_eLwU1r-fhahTG0E%3D)**

America is not obligated to welcome foreign visitors who despise our Western values and seek to undermine our way of life.

clean_body: Communists, Marxists, Islamists, and Socialists want to make us more like Europe. | Why do we continue to import people who hate us, Fellow Patriot? Our immigration system has been cynically used to disadvantage American workers' competitiveness in favor of mass importing from the third world. It's led to higher crime and lower wages. But that's not the only problem. We're importing people who are hostile and downright fundamentally opposed to the American values that make our country great. Just look at what Communists, Marxists, Islamists, and Socialists have done to Europe. We cannot let that happen here at home. We need to stop allowing people to enter the country who are vehemently opposed to the foundation of American values like free-market capitalism. But not just there - we need to DEPORT them, too. America is not obligated to welcome foreign visitors who despise our Western values and seek to undermine our way of life. These are the important fights. We cannot back down, and we need conservative leaders like you to show the DC political class that we aren't the silent majority. name: David Hogg
subject: Citizens United broke our democracy. Here’s how we fix it:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
We’re fighting to end the game dark money and corporate influence have rigged for over a decade with the best tool in our arsenal: people power.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260425-1&t=1&akid=3406%2E587540%2EIirL4u)
Peter,
**Leaders We Deserve is going to be the super PAC to end all super PACs.**
_Citizens United_ has flooded our democracy with corporate cash, dark money, and special interest influence for over a decade. And the only way to fix it permanently is to elect leaders who will fight to overturn it with the urgency it demands.
But until that happens, we can’t send our candidates into races against opponents flush with unlimited corporate money, and tell them to fight with both hands behind their backs. That’s _not_ a winning strategy.
**We play the game we have, and use the tools of a broken system to beat it in its own arena.** And we keep fighting until the system that makes this necessary no longer exists.
That’s exactly what Leaders We Deserve is doing. Backing young, bold progressive candidates who aren’t bought by special interests, who don’t have decades long loyalties to the very corporations they’re supposed to regulate, and will stand up for the working people they represent, no matter what.
And it’s your grassroots support that makes it possible, Peter -- because we don’t take a dime from the special interests we’re fighting against. **Every dollar you give is proof that people power can beat big money.**
**[Will you pitch in $5 today to help Leaders We Deserve take on big money influence and continue backing leaders who will answer to the people they serve, not the highest bidder?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260425_1_pac.Awn0Jn&refcode=em20260425-3406&amount=5&t=2&akid=3406%2E587540%2EIirL4u)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260425_1_pac.Awn0Jn&amount=5&refcode=em20260425-3406&t=21&akid=3406%2E587540%2EIirL4u)**
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20260425-1&t=22&refcode2=3406_587540_IirL4u&akid=3406%2E587540%2EIirL4u)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=23&akid=3406%2E587540%2EIirL4u)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=3406%2E587540%2EIirL4u)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

Friend, There’s a sea change happening in the Democratic Party. In Michigan, statewide nominees are elected at the state Democratic Party Nominating Convention. And we proudly supported five candidates with proven track records of standing up to authoritarianism and demanding our rights. **So often, the political establishment and Big Money dominate party politics―on the state and federal level. But not this year!** All five of our endorsed candidates won their nominations and are now headed to the general election. Read more about these amazing candidates below. **I created Rooted in Community Leadership PAC to demand a politics that puts our communities first, not the billionaires and greedy corporations.** [**In Michigan and across the country, we’re fighting to elect candidates who understand the needs of their communities and who will always put people over special interests. I have fought alongside **Eli Savit** for years as together we’ve taken on polluting corporations, greedy banks, ICE, and the Trump administration.
As the Washtenaw County Prosecutor, Eli has fought back against the Trump administration’s attacks on birthright citizenship, SNAP cuts, brutal ICE tactics, student visas, and attempts to send troops into our cities. He has also protected voting rights, free speech, and workers’ rights.
Eli supported Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison’s lawsuit against ICE. And back in 2021, Eli took the proactive step of ending his county’s collaboration with ICE.
Now, Eli is running to be the next Attorney General for the state of Michigan.
I’m also proud to support **Garlin Gilchrist** as Michigan's next Secretary of State. Garlin is the highest-ranking Black elected official in Michigan history, serving as our Lieutenant Governor. He’s never forgotten his roots; like me, he grew up in Detroit with a parent who worked in the auto industry.
Like me, Garlin stands up to corporate greed in our shared struggle for an economy that puts everyday people first. He’s played a leading role in delivering free Pre-K for all, expanding affordable housing, protecting abortion access, reforming our justice system, and improving access to healthcare and mental health services. He’s also connected tens of thousands of Michiganders to reliable, affordable, high-speed internet.
The Honorable **Tiffany Tilley** has never shied away from advocating for our most vulnerable while also raising the standard for how we invest in education. As the Co-Vice President of the State Board of Education, Tiffany isn’t just a leader in our state. She also sets an example nationally. That’s why, after just one meeting, she was appointed as the representative to the National Association of State Boards of Education.
Tiffany is running for re-election to the State Board of Education to continue advocating to give a voice to underserved populations; fight for increasing equitable funding in an age where so many shy away from Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion; push for more career and technical education programs; address gun violence; increase mental health support; and work to support foster youth across the state.
**Amir Makled** is a civil rights attorney running for University of Michigan Board of Regents. Other board members have prosecuted student protesters and capitulated to Trump. Meanwhile, Amir has provided free legal representation for student protesters.
We need Amir’s voice on the board of regents to defend free speech and academic freedom, including students’ and faculty’s right to speak out against injustice without fear of retaliation or surveillance.
**Shereef Akeel** is a civil rights and human rights attorney running for Wayne State University Board of Governors. Like me, he’s a child of immigrants. He has sued banks, discriminatory employers, Border Patrol, the Department of Homeland Security, and military corporations—and won. If he becomes a member of the Board of Governors, he’ll fight for students’ rights and inclusion at my alma mater.
**Each of these candidates exemplifies how public servants can serve their constituents, not big business and billionaire interests.**

clean_body: Each of these candidates exemplifies how public servants can serve their constituents, not big business and billionaire interests. | | | Friend, There’s a sea change happening in the Democratic Party. In Michigan, statewide nominees are elected at the state Democratic Party Nominating Convention. And we proudly supported five candidates with proven track records of standing up to authoritarianism and demanding our rights. So often, the political establishment and Big Money dominate party politics―on the state and federal level. But not this year! All five of our endorsed candidates won their nominations and are now headed to the general election. Read more about these amazing candidates below. I created Rooted in Community Leadership PAC to demand a politics that puts our communities first, not the billionaires and greedy corporations. | I have fought alongside Eli Savit for years as together we’ve taken on polluting corporations, greedy banks, ICE, and the Trump administration. As the Washtenaw County Prosecutor, Eli has fought back against the Trump administration’s attacks on birthright citizenship, SNAP cuts, brutal ICE tactics, student visas, and attempts to send troops into our cities. He has also protected voting rights, free speech, and workers’ rights. Eli supported Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison’s lawsuit against ICE. And back in 2021, Eli took the proactive step of ending his county’s collaboration with ICE. Now, Eli is running to be the next Attorney General for the state of Michigan. I’m also proud to support Garlin Gilchrist as Michigan's next Secretary of State. Garlin is the highest-ranking Black elected official in Michigan history, serving as our Lieutenant Governor. He’s never forgotten his roots; like me, he grew up in Detroit with a parent who worked in the auto industry. Like me, Garlin stands up to corporate greed in our shared struggle for an economy that puts everyday people first. He’s played a leading role in delivering free Pre-K for all, expanding affordable housing, protecting abortion access, reforming our justice system, and improving access to healthcare and mental health services. He’s also connected tens of thousands of Michiganders to reliable, affordable, high-speed internet. The Honorable Tiffany Tilley has never shied away from advocating for our most vulnerable while also raising the standard for how we invest in education. As the Co-Vice President of the State Board of Education, Tiffany isn’t just a leader in our state. She also sets an example nationally. That’s why, after just one meeting, she was appointed as the representative to the National Association of State Boards of Education. Tiffany is running for re-election to the State Board of Education to continue advocating to give a voice to underserved populations; fight for increasing equitable funding in an age where so many shy away from Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion; push for more career and technical education programs; address gun violence; increase mental health support; and work to support foster youth across the state. Amir Makled is a civil rights attorney running for University of Michigan Board of Regents. Other board members have prosecuted student protesters and capitulated to Trump. Meanwhile, Amir has provided free legal representation for student protesters. We need Amir’s voice on the board of regents to defend free speech and academic freedom, including students’ and faculty’s right to speak out against injustice without fear of retaliation or surveillance. Shereef Akeel is a civil rights and human rights attorney running for Wayne State University Board of Governors. Like me, he’s a child of immigrants. He has sued banks, discriminatory employers, Border Patrol, the Department of Homeland Security, and military corporations—and won. If he becomes a member of the Board of Governors, he’ll fight for students’ rights and inclusion at my alma mater. Each of these candidates exemplifies how public servants can serve their constituents, not big business and billionaire interests. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | Together, we’re demanding a future that includes all of us, not just the wealthy few. Always serving you, Rashida Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . | [Early voting starts this Monday, April 27th — that gives us this weekend to reach as many voters as possible before the first ballots are cast. Chip in $10 to help DPG reach as many voters as possible.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130161196/616820405/-1692272226?&amount=10&source=DPG_EM_FR_042526_B1_EARLYVOTING2_D1_S2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNDRiMzIwOC1jNDQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7RDWX8RuB0_dwlE-BQhALBDHudYl20idEfSwurPl6iM=) This is our chance to win two critical Supreme Court seats and put **Miracle Rankin** and **Jen Jordan** on the bench to be Justices for the people — not the powerful. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130161370/616820591/-1473932214?refcode=logo&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMTRiMzIwOC1jNDQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-0Pu_rMPYuxDFaB9b5b1EEiWdV5CNfOVHbW2jRDVMkY=) It’s safe to say that our to-do list looks a bit different from MAGA Republicans. Take a look: **_MAGA’s To-Do List:_**
* End universal mail-in voting and make it harder for millions of Americans to vote.
* Give $200 BILLION to Trump for his illegal war abroad.
* Pay for more tax cuts for the biggest corporations by making even deeper cuts to health care, housing, and food assistance.
* Expand their MAGA majority and continue pushing Trump’s ruthless agenda.

**_Team Hick’s To-Do List:_**
* Safeguard your fundamental right to vote and prevent Trump from rigging the election.
* Stop Trump’s secret police force and their violence.
* Hold Trump to the Constitution and end his illegal war of choice in Iran.
* Restore funding for Medicaid and SNAP and tackle the cost-of-living emergency.
* Take back our power in Washington and bring Trump’s corruption to an end.

name: Demand Justice PAC
subject: We don’t believe the smokescreens.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260425-61394&t=1001&akid=61394%2E1407376%2EacCUi2 ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260425-61394&t=1002&akid=61394%2E1407376%2EacCUi2 ]Demand Justice PAC

Fox News reported that
Justices Samuel Alito and Clarence Thomas won’t retire this year.

Guess what? We’re not buying it.

When it comes to the Supreme Court under Trump, there’s only one question
that matters: “Who’s in control?”

And if Trump wants them off the bench, they’re off. The idea that he’d
gamble two Supreme Court seats on the outcome of November’s election? Not
a chance.

That’s why we’re treating these reports for what they are: smokescreens.

We’re not waiting. Demand Justice is launching a $3 million campaign to
expose, oppose, and defeat Trump’s SCOTUS shortlist before a vacancy is
even announced.

This fight is about what kind of pressure surrounds that moment from day
one. We’re building a campaign that raises the political cost of a Trump
Court pick so high that Republican senators are forced to answer for it.

That means a rapid-response operation and nationwide pressure designed to
make this confirmation fight as visible, as painful, and as politically
consequential as possible — before it even begins.

It all comes down to one question: Is Trump’s nominee loyal to the
Constitution or loyal to Donald Trump?

That’s the fight ahead. And it’s going to take serious resources to meet
it.

We’re up against a well-funded, decades-long conservative machine that has
reshaped the courts in plain sight. If we’re going to expose what’s
happening — we need to act now.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260425.j3m6TS&refcode=em20260425-61394&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=61394%2E1407376%2EacCUi2 ]So we’re asking: Will you chip in $5 today to help us
get ahead of Trump’s next Supreme Court move and power this fight from day
one?

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260425.j3m6TS&amount=5&refcode=em20260425-61394&t=1022&akid=61394%2E1407376%2EacCUi2 ]Donate $5 ››

Thank you,

Demand Justice PAC

| **About the Republican Party of Florida**
* * *
The Republican Party of Florida will promote the principles upon which our nation and our state were founded: freedom, liberty, personal responsibility, and accountability.

We will advocate fiscally-sound, common-sense solutions that will promote job and economic growth, provide the best education to our children, and create a path to prosperity for Florida and America.

Members of the Republican Party of Florida will be united by these principles and will work to elect Floridians with integrity who will work to enact such solutions. We will seek to be the most effective state party in the nation and serve as a model for other party organizations.

clean_body: We cannot let the man who tried to sabotage President Trump’s agenda do the same to the Sunshine State. | Fellow American, You remember Alex Vindman. He was the "star witness" the Radical Left used to lead the first sham impeachment against President Trump. Now, he’s back—and he’s coming for Florida. The latest reports show Vindman just raised a staggering He isn’t just running a campaign; he’s leading a national Democrat assault to flip Florida Blue and bring the DC circus to our backyard. We cannot let the man who tried to sabotage President Trump’s agenda do the same to the Sunshine State. Vindman has the liberal elite and out-of-state donors in his pocket. Florida is Trump Country, and we need to keep it that way. Thank you, Republican Party of Florida | About the Republican Party of Florida * The Republican Party of Florida will promote the principles upon which our nation and our state were founded: freedom, liberty, personal responsibility, and accountability. We will advocate fiscally-sound, common-sense solutions that will promote job and economic growth, provide the best education to our children, and create a path to prosperity for Florida and America. Members of the Republican Party of Florida will be united by these principles and will work to elect Floridians with integrity who will work to enact such solutions. We will seek to be the most effective state party in the nation and serve as a model for other party organizations. | PAID FOR BY THE REPUBLICAN PARTY OF FLORIDA. Chris Rabb is exactly the kind of leader we need right now.
For over a decade in the Pennsylvania legislature, and as a founding member of the Commonwealth’s Climate Caucus, Chris has been on the frontlines of the fight for climate action.
He pushed for Pennsylvania to achieve 100% clean energy by 2050 with one of the very first bills he introduced in the legislature.
He took on corporate greenwashing, empowering people to sue companies when they lie.
And he fought to expose the true cost of oil drilling so communities see the real damage caused by the fossil fuel industry.
**That’s leadership. Real leadership. And that’s exactly why I’m all in to help him win.**
**[But with 10 other candidates in this race, Chris needs a strong grassroots team beside him right now. Will you chip in $5, or whatever makes sense for your budget, to help power this climate champion to victory?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130161605/616821168/1243420481?amount=5&refcode=ea.60d5fee5-c440-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ms=ea.60d5fee5-c440-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjYwZDVmZWU1LWM0NDAtZjExMS04ZWYyLTAwMGQzYTE0YjY0MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=AXY0dMK9biONLK8rLTjpJ0OGnLAnZW8hYVKosLr8MaA=)**

clean_body: We need your help. | = I’ve spent a lifetime standing up to bullies and taking on the fossil fuel industry, so believe me when I say I can identify a true leader from a mile away. I’m excited to tell you about someone who gives me hope for the future of our country: Chris Rabb. Chris is running for Congress in PA-03 in Philadelphia — the bluest district in the country. That means we have a rare opportunity to send a Democrat who’s beholden to people, not corporations, to Washington. = | = Chris Rabb is exactly the kind of leader we need right now. For over a decade in the Pennsylvania legislature, and as a founding member of the Commonwealth’s Climate Caucus, Chris has been on the frontlines of the fight for climate action. He pushed for Pennsylvania to achieve 100% clean energy by 2050 with one of the very first bills he introduced in the legislature. He took on corporate greenwashing, empowering people to sue companies when they lie. And he fought to expose the true cost of oil drilling so communities see the real damage caused by the fossil fuel industry. That’s leadership. Real leadership. And that’s exactly why I’m all in to help him win. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Onward, xx Jane Fonda | Follow along: | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. We are less than **1 week** away from our April deadline. I want to be clear with you about where we stand and what we are up against. Here are 3 things you need to know:

1\. Trump and House Republicans are on the offensive. They are using their narrow majority to push a brutal anti-immigrant, anti-worker, anti-freedom agenda.

2\. I’m leading the fight to flip the House. As Chair of BOLD PAC, I’m helping elect diverse, progressive Democrats who will stop the MAGA agenda and deliver for working families.

3\. Grassroots power is how we win. While GOP megadonors pour money into battlegrounds, we are building a people-powered movement that can out-organize and outvote them, but only if we hit our goals.

name: MAGA MELTDOWN
subject: “This is NOT supposed to be a close race!”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jamie Davis for U
body:
MAGA is shocked we're competitive. A Black farmer from rural Louisiana isn't supposed to threaten their Senate seat. But here we are.
| | | | | [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001S8KmH3Se8ViN9TQT30SltfhInv2YYIukJRAwMK6t5CrZew66lqO8zDM2DweC8zuHV2YS1wm_VL2RuuSJyVkgEwQK4CkzzzT1fpMADSfn44sTqaXH9KAQk5osfyxKecFcBFjPMagpP5jE9Akh2cy3knUzuycRnv4RiLwhPeXa8H8CMVmjOEHP6m23Kt2cGUsRa92XfBaHm96XjqdZctUsG0yBddoCHwCd&c=LOmMghLDFEPQi4jzhEloHrqnq1ZRxa_AxuUPrcbl2QXPmIi6zfuf5w==&ch=PNjgID1HkUemGU1_9Zfnmmf7axLqeNMjVNSnCFOJFzrLL0X7tOpXgw==)
---
| Peter,
Let me be honest with you about something.
I'm not supposed to be in this race.
The grandson of a sharecropper? Not supposed to be a viable Senate candidate in Louisiana.
A Black farmer from rural Tensas Parish? Not supposed to compete statewide in the Deep South.
Someone without billionaire backers or a Rolodex full of Washington insiders? Not supposed to have a shot at flipping a Senate seat.
But here we are.
And MAGA is absolutely shocked.
They thought Louisiana was locked up. They thought they could focus on defending seats in swing states while the South stayed safely red. They thought a race like ours -- a real populist challenge to their grip on power -- wasn't possible here.
They were wrong.
Look at what's happening: Democrats flipping Trump districts across the South. MAGA candidates tearing each other apart in primaries. Republican retirements at levels not seen since the Great Depression. Trump's approval cratering over his failed Iran war.
This is a once-in-a-lifetime opening. The kind of moment that doesn't come around often in Louisiana politics.
And I believe -- deep in my bones -- that I'm the candidate to meet this moment.
Not because I'm the most polished politician. Not because I've been planning a Senate run my whole career. But because I understand what working Louisianans need because I've lived their struggles.
I know what it's like to watch costs rise while your income stays flat. I know what it's like to compete against corporations that rig the system against family operations. I know what it's like to worry about whether there will be anything left for the next generation.
That's the authentic economic populism that can win here. Not the fake MAGA version that delivers nothing but culture wars -- the real thing that actually fights for working people.
And if we capitalize on this moment -- if we show the political world that Louisiana is genuinely winnable -- everything changes.
Not just for this race. For the entire map. For what's possible in the South.
But only if we have the resources to compete. And that counts on good folks like you stepping up.
[Will you chip in $25, $10, or $5 to help us seize this once-in-a-lifetime opportunity?](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001S8KmH3Se8ViN9TQT30SltfhInv2YYIukJRAwMK6t5CrZew66lqO8zDM2DweC8zuH-4CviLEy89_HSeCxjaSyOi_eT6n9-HFkuA9aysb5D9e6Q42nYEw7PD6m4Cj5vOYKZGNx2BpjytJieE6v3zwyXBAYbX8IEVBIMLLf7BFEd80Y6QZOP8kz7cwRvBZxYJBS5ZO7-bHM1SLKErn30VQ48atZJdJjQ-Ei9vUvvhT_Fjk=&c=LOmMghLDFEPQi4jzhEloHrqnq1ZRxa_AxuUPrcbl2QXPmIi6zfuf5w==&ch=PNjgID1HkUemGU1_9Zfnmmf7axLqeNMjVNSnCFOJFzrLL0X7tOpXgw==)

clean_body: MAGA is shocked we're competitive. A Black farmer from rural Louisiana isn't supposed to threaten their Senate seat. But here we are. | | | | | == | Peter, Let me be honest with you about something. I'm not supposed to be in this race. The grandson of a sharecropper? Not supposed to be a viable Senate candidate in Louisiana. A Black farmer from rural Tensas Parish? Not supposed to compete statewide in the Deep South. Someone without billionaire backers or a Rolodex full of Washington insiders? Not supposed to have a shot at flipping a Senate seat. But here we are. And MAGA is absolutely shocked. They thought Louisiana was locked up. They thought they could focus on defending seats in swing states while the South stayed safely red. They thought a race like ours -- a real populist challenge to their grip on power -- wasn't possible here. They were wrong. Look at what's happening: Democrats flipping Trump districts across the South. MAGA candidates tearing each other apart in primaries. Republican retirements at levels not seen since the Great Depression. Trump's approval cratering over his failed Iran war. This is a once-in-a-lifetime opening. The kind of moment that doesn't come around often in Louisiana politics. And I believe -- deep in my bones -- that I'm the candidate to meet this moment. Not because I'm the most polished politician. Not because I've been planning a Senate run my whole career. But because I understand what working Louisianans need because I've lived their struggles. I know what it's like to watch costs rise while your income stays flat. I know what it's like to compete against corporations that rig the system against family operations. I know what it's like to worry about whether there will be anything left for the next generation. That's the authentic economic populism that can win here. Not the fake MAGA version that delivers nothing but culture wars -- the real thing that actually fights for working people. And if we capitalize on this moment -- if we show the political world that Louisiana is genuinely winnable -- everything changes. Not just for this race. For the entire map. For what's possible in the South. But only if we have the resources to compete. And that counts on good folks like you stepping up. == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | == | I wasn't supposed to be here. But I am. And we're going to shock the world. Jamie Davis Democrat for U.S. Senate | | == | | | == | == Mail checks to: Jamie Davis for U.S. Senate PO Box 55 Ferriday, LA 71334 Paid for by Jamie Davis for U.S. Senate | | | | PO Box 55 | Ferriday, LA 71334 US | |

Friend, it’s Xavier. I’m not sending this email out to millions of people. It’s only going to a smaller number of folks like you who have stood by my side when I needed you most. And I’m hoping you’re someone who can step up once again to help me today. You’ve heard it from me a few times in the last few days, but it bears repeating. Momentum is on our side. We’re surging in the polls — our support nearly TRIPLED in just one week. This is a huge moment for our campaign. Everyone in California is watching — the press, political pundits, and our opponents. And no matter how they feel about it, one thing is becoming clear to them: we have a great chance of winning this race. But we still have work to do if we are going to win the primary on June 2nd and advance to the general election. And it's going to take resources — lots of them. That’s where you come in. Knowing I have you on the grassroots team means a great deal to me. I am proud to run a campaign that is powered by people, giving what they can to defend our state from Donald Trump and his allies, and who know the importance of California having a governor with experience and that they can trust to fight for them on Day One. So right now, I am turning to you for support. If you can contribute to my campaign today, it will help us maintain our momentum in this race and help us build the resources we need to win this primary. * * *
**** Felon, fraud, and scandalous creep Donald Trump is at it again. Despite overwhelming public opposition, he is charging ahead with his latest vanity project: a 250-foot "Triumphal Arch" in D.C.

Didn’t Trump just tell us there is no money for daycare, Medicare, and Medicaid - only war? What else did he take away from us so he could build the Arc de Trump? He calls it a "triumphal" celebration. We call it an EPIC FAILURE. clean_body: | | | STOP TRUMP’S VANITY ARCH! Felon, fraud, and scandalous creep Donald Trump is at it again. Despite overwhelming public opposition, he is charging ahead with his latest vanity project: a 250-foot "Triumphal Arch" in D.C. Didn’t Trump just tell us there is no money for daycare, Medicare, and Medicaid - only war? What else did he take away from us so he could build the Arc de Trump? He calls it a "triumphal" celebration. We call it an EPIC FAILURE. While American families struggle and our nation spirals downward, Trump is focused on ripping off the people to build monuments to himself. He is slapping his putrid stain of a name on everything he can, turning our nation’s capital into a personal branding opportunity. We cannot let this "Arc de Trump" become his legacy. Hey, Trump - we’ve got some monuments to you - Cedar Key Progress is fighting back. We are expanding our massive billboard campaign to expose his corruption and working tirelessly to ELECT a Congress with the backbone to impeach him. He thinks he’s untouchable. Patriot,
---
It's Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13 _ _ and The Green Mile or the CBS television series CSI: NY - but it was in 1994 playing the role of Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. **But I've never been more excited to serve as an Honorary Grand Marshall than I am this year - the year of America's 250****th**** birthday.**

On Memorial Day - not just this momentous year of military milestones, but every year - please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. I believe that while we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, we can always do a little more. **Patriot, thank you for your support of America 's heroes. ** [**And please, if you’re able, make a gift to fund the National Memorial Day parade right now during America’s 250****th****birthday.**](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=8beaab88ae5742ca85f1f0e45838e7e3&_e=27-lgja9lDWwAhusOIjH9TiIxeuNhpZw3GAnc5afgyZXlfIF4RPXQyyJoluGqL0uDmey5V-nSN2N7rVmHvRosv_G8K5NAVjkVzCh--pD4cfQ8d-1VugAW5GRAfXNEyyBXbsTQqppJKSQoLCSNzcnkPqXjN-ykKoqWoY40_HBcZGOwlHGSGJxCDb0qhie95HVYW6Kslh1hE0-FeoA9sX5lBhAID2w2D00GYUmGH6dGyimxz48tfz3rTkj2Qg4Vhf0SYrFcq-xZ8g_cgqUYXK2uF1diClqdUFR-6MuurmaSTavAh0ry1z-fHbrdtJfBqiPjSdbTKTCGZEgSxmT4IhSzuBAuBNWIX0JBREFKm7ttXHUSKFnCE8EjCqJMaCvNfHTUFJFzu-vkCv4Huw1cKbf-6J7m1sdgoU_nWW6VgLHf04taB4WfuChCdwuWe4Aky7kRJrq9QAXCIJn2Q_xNSXT6yw6p8qk8CMWqht8phaU3u47QRLnPxH6HAY-D1CnaKP7EF76o7orlwlQFlK72wqJShZIvhOjVcnv8yE4TeIY1mNw_ROa5ylXlXp6FsjzLFRgcMaIcq3jJ_AvhWZVcHYweTHBpjq8OAgbbVkubpP4_JM4UPjUPfMxF0bsKC1bAXXHfRFQvtB4dxL9f94C_9jv8dhd4gdWFqwm1kwFhXxNlywCvuAEIbEQdZJlbMZ7mwQdAtPURCiKIGFMkhrFmSfydA%3D%3D)

The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. clean_body: A historic opportunity to honor veterans this Memorial Day | | | Patriot, It's Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13 and The Green Mile or the CBS television series CSI: NY - but it was in 1994 playing the role of Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. But I've never been more excited to serve as an Honorary Grand Marshall than I am this year - the year of America's 250th birthday. Patriot, this Memorial Day marks the first major national act of remembrance of the veterans who made the ultimate sacrifice to defend America from its enemies and ensure it has remained the Land of the Free for the last 250 years. And this year's National Memorial Day Parade will be a special edition - the biggest parade yet - where national memory meets living history as veterans from every military generation celebrate America and honor the service and sacrifice of their fallen brethren. Patriot, the National Memorial Day Parade is ENIRELY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous patriots like you. The 2026 National Memorial Day Parade is a once-in-a-lifetime moment where we need to do all we can to honor our country and our veterans. Bigger crowds. More veterans. More young people learning what courage, service, and sacrifice really mean. Historically, AVC has been able to do this at a fraction of what other major public events cost to put on each year. In fact, the entire parade costs about as much as just THREE balloons in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in the Rose Bowl Parade! That means that every dollar that's contributed by a generous American like you makes a difference for this important event. But I want to reiterate that American Veterans Center does not take ANY federal funding for this event. It is funded entirely by private citizens like you who want to honor those who have sacrificed so much for our country. | | Your support of the American Veterans Center not only helps the parade, but also supports great programs like: Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans from World War II to today before we lose these stories forever. Hold our annual American Veterans Conference, which allows young cadets a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from WWII, Korea, Vietnam, and our modern wars. Produce veteran-focused T.V. documentaries and specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom. But of course, the National Memorial Day Parade is AVC's flagship event honoring America's veterans. And this year's parade will be the largest in their history of hosting the parade. If you've been looking for an opportunity to give back to those who have given so much for our freedom, this is one of the best possible ways you could do it. And for a special group of veterans, this might be one of the last chances we get. You see, with each year that goes by, we lose more and more of our World War II veterans, and for the few we have remaining, traveling to Washington, D.C., for the parade is difficult. | | | | | WWII veterans were a key part of the first parade when it was revived in 2005, as they have been every year since for the last 20 years. But this may be the last year we have the opportunity to have these brave men and women with us for the parade. This year is a rare moment when living witnesses to World War II and America 's 250**th birthday overlap. And no matter how much you can give to this important cause, I want to ask something additional of you. On Memorial Day - not just this momentous year of military milestones, but every year - please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. I believe that while we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, we can always do a little more. Patriot, thank you for your support of America 's heroes. Sincerely, Gary Sinise Honarary Grand MarshalNational Memorial Day Parade P.S. Patriot, America is stepping into its 250th year, and this Memorial Day begins that national remembrance. Veterans across generations have given their lives to defend America and all she stands for. And for the Greatest Generation in particular, this may be the last time they're able to join us at this year's National Memorial Day Parade. There's no better send-off we can give them than to make sure they're present to honor their fallen brethren during America's 250th birthday. The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. (C) American Veterans Center 1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201

name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: A new name doesn’t fix BS
body:
|
#
Friend, Remember last year, when Republicans in Congress passed their extreme, partisan bill to kick 15 million people off their health care, take food benefits away from kids and seniors, and add $4+ trillion to the national debt – all so they could hand huge tax cuts to billionaires? Well, working people remember. And they aren’t happy. So now? Republicans went to the House Floor again to pass a resolution rebranding their bill the “Working Families Tax Credit.” That’s BS. We’re not going to waste time pretending disastrous policy is something it’s not. If Republicans were genuinely serious about delivering real working class tax relief, they’d be looking at the bill I just introduced: **The Working Parents Tax Relief Act.** | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130162442/616823904/846650056?refcode=em-4.24.26-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3YjkyNzczOS1jODQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bZQk7YsuG0Cv70WSrUZ-nHXWQnqz4uxBJFMR1rtoDPU=)
**Parents could see $5,500 boost to earned income tax credit under new Democratic measure**
_“Our bill puts thousands of hard-earned dollars back in their pockets, helping parents keep up with their bills while raising their families.” **McDonald Rivet said.**_** **
---
This is about putting money directly back in your pocket – especially at the most financially stressful point of parents’ lives, when you’re bringing home a new baby, and when you’re raising little kids and trying to make it all work.
My bill expands the Earned Income Tax Credit so working parents will see up to $5,500 back per kid. That’s what real tax relief is, friend.
And I know how much that matters – having raised six kids, I know what it's like to bring that kid home from the hospital and begin to wonder how to make it all work.
I’m in Congress to fight for families like yours. But continuing that fight means protecting this seat in one of the most competitive districts in the country.

subject: Trump’s latest deportation scheme

body:
Trump and his mass deportation scheme must be stopped. |
---
Peter, The Trump administration is taking a shocking next step in its lawless mass deportation scheme — criminal prosecutions of naturalized citizens to expedite their deportation. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130162712/616824550/-1870630518?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20260425_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDkyNzczOS1jODQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JGHV6Xv9AXMOItezSKg0aPGCy8coase5AR9UsXJgUVI=) The _New York Times_ reported the Department of Justice has identified 384 foreign-born Americans from whom it wants to strip citizenship. Those are people who have become U.S. citizens through the legal process of naturalization after meeting requirements such as lawful permanent residency, English proficiency, knowledge of U.S. history and government, and good moral character. The DOJ is assigning the cases to prosecutors in dozens of U.S. attorneys’ offices around the country. By assigning these cases to regular prosecutors across the country, they are bypassing decades of legal tradition to fast-track a political agenda. This is another blatant abuse of power by using the DOJ as a tool for intimidation and division between U.S. and foreign-born citizens. This sends a clear message to the millions of naturalized Americans: **_Your status as citizens and the lives you have built in this country are not secure._** That’s exactly what Trump and his shadow president, Stephen Miller, want to achieve. They want anyone they consider a “non-American” to live in fear and ultimately, to leave the country. They’ll stop at nothing. Consider the recent case in which they targeted the new wife of a U.S. Army staff sergeant. The pair had only been married a week, and was starting the process of getting her green card when she was detained. **This is not my America, and I don’t think it’s yours, either.** **[Congress has to put a stop to these cruel mass deportation policies. Lives are at stake. It’s vital that we flip the House in November to provide a backstop to Trump’s lawless administration. Will you chip in $5 or whatever you can to help us?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130162713/616824552/-1870630518?amount=5&refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20260425_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDkyNzczOS1jODQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JGHV6Xv9AXMOItezSKg0aPGCy8coase5AR9UsXJgUVI=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $5 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130162714/616824554/-1870630518?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20260425_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDkyNzczOS1jODQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JGHV6Xv9AXMOItezSKg0aPGCy8coase5AR9UsXJgUVI=)

This is not my America, and I don’t think it’s yours, either. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thanks for standing with us. — Dan Dan Goldman for New York PO BOX 3306 New York, NY 10008 United States | mailto:info@dangoldmanforny.com | Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York

**** The headlines are devastating: 80% of top leadership positions at the CDC remain vacant. Under the current administration and Robert F. Kennedy Jr., critical public health work has ground to a standstill. This leadership vacuum doesn't just stall paperwork, it endangers the lives of millions, especially the disability community. At Able Dems, we know that when the CDC is gutted, our community is the first to suffer. We cannot allow this assault on science and accessibility to continue. **We are fighting to elect Democrats with disabilities and the allies who will defend our right to healthcare, safety, and representation. To stop Trump’s dangerous agenda and restore sanity to our federal agencies, we must take back Congress.** [**We can’t do this alone. If you ever doubted we could win America's youth back from the Left...

If you ever wondered whether we'd lost the next generation for good...

If you ever sat up at night -- watching the news, seeing what's happening in our schools, our culture, our country -- and thought, _" Is it already too late?" _

You're not alone.

A lot of good people have been asking the same question.

**But I want to tell you something that might just change how you feel about the future of this country... **

Just one year ago, Turning Point USA's high school program -- which is now called "Club America" -- was in about 1,200 schools.

That was no small feat.

Starting a single chapter today takes serious courage. Students get mocked. Threatened. Investigated. Sometimes even physically attacked.

So reaching 1,200 chapters already felt like a miracle.

_Then Charlie called me... _

He said: "Andrew, I love what we're doing with our high school program -- but we've got to move faster."

I told him, "We are."

Charlie replied, "No you're not thinking what I'm thinking... I want to be in 35,000 high schools."

I laughed. I told him, "Charlie, there aren't even 35,000 high schools in America."

"Okay," he said, "How many are there?"

"About 27,000."

Without skipping a beat, Charlie said, "Okay, great. Let's do 27,000!"
I'll be honest -- I froze.

We didn't have the numbers. We didn't have the staff. We didn't have the roadmap yet.

But he wasn't fazed.

He said, "Don't worry. We'll figure it out. We'll do it together."

That was Charlie.

He didn't think small. He didn't move slow. He didn't settle for "maybe someday."

He believed in going now.

And he believed that if the mission was right, the path would reveal itself.

He chased what others only dreamed about... and turned what others thought impossible into reality.

That's why he built Turning Point USA in the first place.

That's why tens of thousands of students were inspired by Charlie to start chapters of their own -- _many in schools where they were explicitly told not to. _

**And that 's why today -- less than a year later -- we're not in 1,200 schools anymore: **

And that fight has never been more urgent than it is right now. Patriot, if you 've ever wondered whether one small act could change the direction of our country -- this is it. You may not be able to step into a hostile high school and stand for truth like these students do. But you can send them the tools to do it. These students are ready. They've raised their hands. They chose courage and decided to lead. All they're waiting on... is us. Every $25 helps send an activism kit to a young patriot and gives them everything they need to win hearts and minds. You're gift reminds them they're not alone. It gives them the confidence to stand and fight back. And you'll be saving our country -- one future freedom fighter at a time. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

body:
|

Friend,

The last few weeks have been deeply impactful – both at home in Illinois and in Washington.

I want to pull back the curtain on exactly how I’ve been fighting for you, but before I do, I need to humbly ask for **[your support with $3](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130163145/616825964/-1967985835?refcode=em-4.25.26-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlY2E3ODA5Ni1jODQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=q9UWic-VnmQh5nuxu78y4X7CoIh-QE5eTiQyeBjiZLY=)** ahead of the end-of-month deadline.

I was proud of the results of our March primary election. Voters showed up at the ballot box and made their voices heard. We knocked on thousands of doors, attended local events, and listened to what matters to Illinoisans.

In Washington, I introduced the PREDICT Act – because government officials have no business using inside information to trade on the stock market or prediction markets.

It’s the latest example of corruption running rampant in our democracy, and I won’t stand for it. The American people deserve better than distrust.

I’m also continuing to push back against the MAGA agenda stripping away so much from working families – from affordable healthcare to rising costs at the gas pump and the grocery store.

**[As we move closer to November, we’re expecting an influx of national GOP attention on this race. Only a few seats separate House control, and we can’t afford to lose – will you chip in $3 to make an immediate impact today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130163147/616825967/-1967985835?refcode=em-4.25.26-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlY2E3ODA5Ni1jODQwLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=q9UWic-VnmQh5nuxu78y4X7CoIh-QE5eTiQyeBjiZLY=)**

clean_body: | = Friend, The last few weeks have been deeply impactful – both at home in Illinois and in Washington. I want to pull back the curtain on exactly how I’ve been fighting for you, but before I do, I need to humbly ask for = ahead of the end-of-month deadline. I was proud of the results of our March primary election. Voters showed up at the ballot box and made their voices heard. We knocked on thousands of doors, attended local events, and listened to what matters to Illinoisans. In Washington, I introduced the PREDICT Act – because government officials have no business using inside information to trade on the stock market or prediction markets. It’s the latest example of corruption running rampant in our democracy, and I won’t stand for it. The American people deserve better than distrust. I’m also continuing to push back against the MAGA agenda stripping away so much from working families – from affordable healthcare to rising costs at the gas pump and the grocery store. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thank you, Nikki | = = = Nikki is a champion for working families – from leading the fight to pass a $15 minimum wage in Illinois, to bringing manufacturing back to America. name: Paul and Betsy
subject: Our mom
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
A lot of politicians talk about things that don’t really affect kids or people our age. But what our mom talks about actually does.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=41217%2E3422442%2EpJaq6L)
Hey Peter!
This is Paul and Betsy, two of Katie Porter’s kids.
We’ve seen how hard our mom works and how much she cares about California for as long as we can remember—and now she’s working just as hard running for governor.
A lot of politicians talk about things that feel pretty disconnected from kids or people our age. **But what our mom talks about actually affects our lives.**
We grew up in California, and we love it here. But honestly, it’s hard not to worry that we won’t be able to afford to stay here once we’re adults.
That’s a big reason our mom is running.
She’s fighting for universal child care, making college tuition-free, and helping kids like us own a home some day. Basically, she wants our next generation to have a real shot at building a life here in California.
We’ve seen her travel all over California and talk to people, and it’s always the same story: _families are going through exactly what we are, but no one is listening._
But our mom has always been someone who listens—and actually does something about it. That’s what she’s trying to do for California too.
We’re really proud of how hard she’s working on this campaign. And we’re helping out however we can by going to events or helping with laundry back home.
Her winning takes all of us and is a full family effort, which is why we wanted to reach out to you!
**[We’d be honored if you would chip in $5 or more to our mom’s campaign for governor. She’ll use those resources to keep fighting for Californians like us.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260425_1_ak.rQhH9Q&refcode=em20260425-41217&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=41217%2E3422442%2EpJaq6L)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260425_1_ak.rQhH9Q&refcode=em20260425-41217&t=22&akid=41217%2E3422442%2EpJaq6L)
Thank you!
Paul and Betsy

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20260425-41217&amounts=&t=23&refcode2=41217_3422442_pJaq6L&akid=41217%2E3422442%2EpJaq6L)**
